name: Norwegian Hazelnut Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease the bottom only of a 10-inch springform pan.
3. Set aside 12 whole nut pieces for garnish.
4. In a blender or food processor with a metal blade, grind nuts (about 1 1/3 cups); set aside 1 tablespoon ground nuts for garnish.
5. Melt butter and let cool.
6. In a large bowl, beat eggs, sugar, and 1 teaspoon vanilla until thick and lemon-colored, about 2-3 minutes.
7. Sift flour into a measuring cup; level off.
8. Add flour, baking powder, and ground nuts to the bowl; mix well.
9. Gradually add cooled melted butter until well blended (mixture will be thick).
10. Pour batter into prepared pan.
11. Bake for 35-45 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
12. Let cool for 15 minutes, then remove the sides of the pan.
13. Run a long knife under the cake at the bottom; invert onto a serving plate.
14. Cover with a cloth towel and let cool for about 30 minutes.
15. To prepare glaze, in a medium saucepan, bring whipping cream and salt to a boil; remove from heat.
16. Stir in chocolate chips and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla until chips are melted and smooth.
17. Pour glaze over the top of the cake, allowing some to drizzle down the sides.
18. Sprinkle reserved ground nuts around the top edge of the cake.
19. Arrange reserved whole nuts around the top edge of the cake on top of the ground nuts.
